A Long and Considered Headline About Tariffs

Tariffs are taxes imposed by a government on imported goods and services. They have been used for centuries to protect domestic industry, raise revenue, and apply political pressure in international trade disputes.

Economists generally agree that while tariffs can shield local producers in the short run, the costs are usually passed on to domestic consumers in the form of higher prices for the affected products and their inputs.
